raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"A general request to review a research section centrally includes checking whether the stated motivation, method, results, ablation, and conclusion form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The section makes quantitative performance and ablation claims, so review centrally includes checking whether the claims are aligned with the reported experimental evidence.","R1.6":"Because the delegation targets a section rather than a full paper, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and flow are central expected responsibilities.","RX.1":"Any review projection should account for uncertainty where the artifact lacks external context or full experimental details.","RX.2":"The section includes broad claims about physical priors and other PDE systems, so avoiding overclaiming is actively relevant.","RX.3":"The reviewer must stay within reviewing the provided section rather than executing edits or evaluating unavailable materials.","RX.4":"The claims concern scientific performance and applicability, so the projection includes avoiding downstream harm from unsupported technical assertions.","RX.5":"A review should preserve traceability to the provided text and reported numbers when identifying responsibilities."}}

rationale.R1.2: A general request to review a research section centrally includes checking whether the stated motivation, method, results, ablation, and conclusion form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes quantitative performance and ablation claims, so review centrally includes checking whether the claims are aligned with the reported experimental evidence.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation targets a section rather than a full paper, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and flow are central expected responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: Any review projection should account for uncertainty where the artifact lacks external context or full experimental details.
rationale.RX.2: The section includes broad claims about physical priors and other PDE systems, so avoiding overclaiming is actively relevant.
rationale.RX.3: The reviewer must stay within reviewing the provided section rather than executing edits or evaluating unavailable materials.
rationale.RX.4: The claims concern scientific performance and applicability, so the projection includes avoiding downstream harm from unsupported technical assertions.
rationale.RX.5: A review should preserve traceability to the provided text and reported numbers when identifying responsibilities.
